title: Ajania adenantha
taxonomy: Ajania adenantha is a species within the family Compositae, now more commonly referred to as Asteraceae. This classification places it within the plant order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. At the genus level, Ajania adenantha is classified as a member of the genus Ajania, a group of flowering plants. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group, comprising many different species of flowering plants. Further classification within the plant kingdom would include the division Magnoliophyta and the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Ajania adenantha, also known as Chinese wormwood, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ajania adenantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Ajania adenantha is often used to make teas, infusions, and herbal decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin issues and wound healing.
